mnemonics: Think PIPE. Pipe (|) connects Internal and External commands to build powerful combinations.
keyPoints: Internal commands (builtins) are part of the shell itself and execute faster (e.g., cd, echo, pwd).
External commands are separate executable programs stored in directories like /bin or /usr/bin (e.g., ls, grep, cat).
The pipe operator (|) allows you to combine commands by using the output of one as the input to the next, enabling complex operations.
commonMistakes: Assuming all commands are external and not knowing which are built-in shell commands.
Forgetting that pipes connect stdout of one command to stdin of the next, and errors (stderr) are not piped by default.
Using a pipe without ensuring the first command produces the correct output format for the second command to process.
